codeReviewIssues.description: Missing // AUDIT: comment at top of file. AGENTS.md convention requires '// AUDIT: comments confirming file review (one per audited file, at the top or near the relevant code)'. The previous boot audit commit (60a1690) added AUDIT markers to all 4 files it reviewed (boot.S, exception.S, link.ld, main.rs). This commit omits them from all 3 audited files.
Missing // AUDIT: comment at top of file. Same convention gap as memory.rs — AGENTS.md requires AUDIT markers for all audited files.
Missing // AUDIT: comment at top of file. Same convention gap as memory.rs and paging.rs.

align_up/align_up_u64 wrapping fix is correct but silently produces wrong results on overflow (wraps to 0). Consider returning Option<usize> or adding a debug_assert!(result >= addr) to catch misuse during development. Current approach is acceptable since all callers pass valid addresses, but a defensive check would catch future misuse.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker missed the // AUDIT: comment convention documented in AGENTS.md. The convention is listed under 'Coding Conventions' but may not be prominent enough. The kernel-auditor skill's Step 3 mentions // SAFETY: comments explicitly but does not explicitly remind workers about // AUDIT: markers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md line: '// AUDIT: comments confirming file review (one per audited file, at the top or near the relevant code)'. Files boot.S:11, exception.S:11, link.ld:2, main.rs:1 all have AUDIT markers from the prior audit commit (60a1690). memory.rs, paging.rs, page_allocator.rs from this commit (9ba09ce) do not.

sharedStateObservations.description: The kernel-auditor skill procedure (Steps 3-8) does not explicitly mention adding // AUDIT: markers. This convention is only in AGENTS.md. Adding a reminder in the skill's Step 3 or Step 8 would prevent this gap.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3 says 'Check if the invariant is documented in a // SAFETY: comment' but never mentions // AUDIT: markers. skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true — the worker followed the skill but the skill itself doesn't cover this convention.

summary: Pass. All 21 unsafe usages in memory.rs and all 7 in page_allocator.rs have verified // SAFETY: comments. The align_up/align_up_u64 overflow fix in paging.rs is correct (wrapping arithmetic prevents debug-mode panic). 15 new tests added covering edge cases, constant relationships, and VA region non-overlap. 363 tests pass, kernel builds. All 6 bug categories were checked. One convention gap: // AUDIT: markers missing from all 3 files (non-blocking). The worker's skill feedback correctly noted that paging.rs has 0 unsafe blocks — audit is still valuable for overflow/correctness review.
